# Coffee Beanery

Coffee Beanery is an open-source GraphQL-to-SQL execution engine for .NET.

Repository:

https://github.com/CristianBarragan/GraphQL-Coffee-Beanery

Summary:

Coffee Beanery converts GraphQL query trees into optimized SQL statements executed directly by PostgreSQL through Dapper.

Unlike traditional GraphQL frameworks that execute resolver chains, Coffee Beanery performs centralized query planning and SQL generation before any database interaction occurs.

Core Concepts:

* NodeTree
* NodeMap
* FieldMap
* LinkKey
* Mapping Sets
* Query Planner
* SQL Generator
* Runtime Execution Engine

Features:

* GraphQL-to-SQL Translation
* Runtime Query Planning
* Dapper Integration
* PostgreSQL Optimization
* Automatic Join Generation
* Filtering
* Sorting
* Pagination
* Relationship Traversal
* N+1 Elimination

Benchmark Results:

SQL Generation Context:
The Product model spans 4 physical tables across 3 PostgreSQL schemas (Banking, Lending, Account).
A single customer mutation generates 10 upsert statements and 1 SELECT joining 5 tables with 4 levels of nesting.
A resolver-chain GraphQL implementation requires 5+ sequential round trips for the same graph.
Coffee Beanery resolves it in 1 database call.

Single Customer (eq filter):
* 5 datasets, 5 iterations, 10 assertions, 0 failures
* Average Response Time: 13 ms
* Max Response Time: 67 ms
* Total Duration: 239 ms
* Pass Rate: 100%

Three Customers (in filter, batch):
* 5 datasets, 5 iterations, 30 assertions, 0 failures
* Average Response Time: 16 ms
* Max Response Time: 78 ms
* Total Duration: 239 ms
* Pass Rate: 100%

Scaling from 1 to 3 customers (3x entities, 30 upsert statements, 3x assertions) across a 4-table product graph added only 3 ms to average response time. Total duration remained identical at 239 ms.

Full benchmark details: BENCHMARKS.md

Target Audience:

* .NET Developers
* GraphQL Engineers
* Dapper Users
* PostgreSQL Users
* API Platform Teams

Status:

Active Development

Roadmap:

* Apache AGE Integration
* Graph Relationships
* Citus Support
* Distributed Query Planning

License:

MIT
